page_content:
ENGL311 17th- and 18th-Century British Literature and Culture (3 Credits)

Explore 17th- and 18th-century British literary cultures. Read novels, political writing, poetry and drama by authors such as Milton, Behn, Swift, Equiano, and Wollstonecraft. Learn about the history of empire, colonialism, coffee house culture, female performance, science, philosophy, sexuality, and revolution.

ENGL312 Romantic to Modern British Literature and Culture (3 Credits) Explore 19th- and 20th-century British and Anglophone literary cultures, including Romanticism, Victorianism, and Modernism. We will read poetry, prose, and drama by authors such as Mary and Percy Shelley

to Austen, Dickens, Eliot, Tagore, and Woolf. Learn about the history of empire and resistance, the industrial revolution, capitalism, communism, feminism, race, and new forms of literary and artistic creation.
